paper_overview.citation: Verma, N., Singh, G. & Ahsan, N. (2023). Assessment of Spatiotemporal Variations in Water Quality of the Urban River Reach, Yamuna, Delhi. Water Air Soil Pollut, 234(571). https://doi.org/10.1007/s11270-023-06569-1
paper_overview.study_region: Urban River Reach, Yamuna, Delhi, India

data_sources.location: Real-Time Water Quality Monitoring Stations (RTWQMS) of the Delhi Pollution Control Committee (DPCC) at Wazirabad and Okhla
data_sources.description: Real-time water quality data collected from two monitoring stations (Wazirabad and Okhla) using S::CAN water monitoring technologies (UV Spectro::lyser, Ammino::lyser Eco, pH::lyser).

sites.description: Upstream monitoring station on the Yamuna River, Wazirabad
sites.source_quote: The data were collected from two Real-Time Water Quality Monitoring Stations (RTWQMS) of the Delhi Pollution Control Committee (DPCC) upstream of the Wazirabad (ST1) barrage and upstream of the Okhla (ST2) barrage.

sites.description: Downstream monitoring station on the Yamuna River, Okhla
sites.source_quote: The data were collected from two Real-Time Water Quality Monitoring Stations (RTWQMS) of the Delhi Pollution Control Committee (DPCC) upstream of the Wazirabad (ST1) barrage and upstream of the Okhla (ST2) barrage.

temporal_coverage.granularity: Hourly for ST1 (6085 observations), 15-min intervals for ST2 (25,540 observations)

extraction_notes: The paper states 11 parameters were assessed. For ST1, data for May 2021 was not available. Latitude and longitude for sites were converted from degrees, minutes, seconds to decimal degrees.
